data.title: Indian textile sector could see a festive cheer amidst disruptions in competing market, growing demand
data.description:
China has been losing market share worldwide with rising labour costs and China+1’ theme playing out

  • Watchlist
  • Portfolio
  • Message
  • Set Alert
 
 
live
  • bselive
  • nselive
Volume
Todays L/H
More

India’s textile sector could be poised for significant growth as disruptions in competing markets open up new opportunities for Indian exporters, according to a recent report by JM Financial.

Bangladesh, once a dominant player in global textile supply chains, is grappling with political instability and supply chain disruptions. Meanwhile, rising operational costs in Vietnam are further complicating matters. These challenges could drive global retailers to increasingly turn to India as a reliable sourcing alternative.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

The report highlights that India’s market share in apparel exports to the US and UK has grown steadily, reaching 7 percent and 6 percent, respectively, in 2024, up from 6 percent and 5 percent in 2023.

Better demand, Better margins

Indian players like Gokaldas Exports and Indocount expect relatively better demand in 2H compared to 1H due to a possible renewed appetite for buying from retailers on the back of the festival season and now that the global inventory de-stocking cycle has now come to an end, according to the report.

The elevated freight costs due to peak season shipments and the Red Sea issue are expected to cool off in the coming months, which will aid margins for Indian players.

The recently signed UK-India Free Trade Agreement (FTA), could also be beneficial to Indian exporters.

In the US, India’s market share in cotton sheet imports rose to 61 percent in 2024, up from 59 percent last year, despite a slight dip in September and challenges like the Red Sea crisis, and supply chain and logistics issues. Overall, US retailers are seeing a mixed demand sentiment. For example, Walmart raised its full-year revenue guidance to 4.8 percent-5.1 percent. On the other hand, competitors like Nike and Target faced softer consumer demand.

Another benefit to Indian textile manufacturers could be the stabilising of prices. According to the report, earlier in 2024, cotton and yarn prices peaked due to supply chain disruptions, higher demand, or market uncertainties. However, by November 2024, prices normalized, with cotton priced at Rs 151 per kg and yarn at Rs 232 per kg. The report notes that the spread between Indian and Chinese cotton prices has also narrowed significantly.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Mixed trends for global retailers

According to the report, global retailers reported mixed inventory trends during their latest earnings calls. Walmart on the back of strong Q3 2024 results, raised its full-year revenue guidance to 4.8%-5.1% from 3.75%-4.75%. While Target and Nike expect continued demand softness, TJX remains optimistic about home textiles. Overall, the report sees that global demand is expected to see a slight boost driven by the ongoing holiday season.

Boost for Indian stocks

Indian textile stocks have been gaining on the back of expected growth in demand. On December 9, stocks of Gokaldas, Trident, Indo Count and Welspun gained nearly 14 percent through the day with Gokaldas closing 6 percent higher, Trident closing 10 percent higher and Welspun closing 5 percent higher at the end of trading for the day.

Disclaimer: The views and investment tips expressed by investment experts on Moneycontrol.com are their own and not those of the website or its management. Moneycontrol.com advises users to check with certified experts before taking any investment decisions.

data.title: SEBI warns against unregulated platforms that facilitate trade in unlisted securities of public limited companies
data.description:
Only recognized stock exchanges are authorised to provide a platform for fund raising and trading in securities of “to be listed” and “listed” companies, said the regulator's press release.

The market regulator has cautioned investors against platforms that facilitate transactions in unlisted securities of public limited companies.

In a press statement issued on December 9, the Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI) said, "Such activities are in violation of Securities Contract (Regulation) Act, 1956 and SEBI Act, 1992 which are, inter alia, laws designed to regulate and protect the interest of investors in securities market."

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

The regulator referred to an earlier press statement, from 2016, when it had cautioned investors from interacting with such platforms. The 2016 circualr siad, "It has come to the notice of SEBI that various entities are soliciting investors by offering leagues/schemes/competitions etc. related to securities markets. Some of the schemes may involve distribution of prize monies. Participation in such schemes including sharing of confidential and personal trading data is at investors’ own risk, cost and consequences as such schemes are neither approved nor endorsed by SEBI / SEBI recognized Exchange(s)."

Also read: "Ignorance of law cannot be an excuse": SEBI fines Premier Polyfilm for related-party transactions

In this 2016 statement, SEBI had also warned against interacting with platforms that facilitate fundraising that look like private placement but are in violation of SEBI regulations; and had warned against dealing with unregistered investment advisors and research analysts.

In the latest statement too, SEBI asked investors to not share any sensitive personal details on such platforms, which are not authorised or recognised by the regulator.

The press statement said, "Only recognized stock exchanges are authorised to provide a platform for fund raising and trading in securities of “to be listed” and “listed” companies. The details of SEBI recognized stock exchanges are available on the SEBI website www.sebi.gov.in."
data.author: MoneyControl

data.title: Dhanlaxmi Crop Science IPO fully booked at 17.24x on day 1
data.description:
Dhanlaxmi Crop Science IPO

Dhanlaxmi Crop Science's initial public offering has attracted strong interest from investors on the first day of bidding, December 9, getting subscribed 17.24 times with investors buying 5.46 crore equity shares against the offer size of 31.7 lakh shares.

The seeds producer is planning to mobilise Rs 23.8 crore through an initial share sale of 43.28 lakh shares at the upper end of price band of Rs 52-55 per share.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Retail investors were at the forefront to boost subscription numbers, bidding 31.33 times the reserved portion, followed by non-institutional investors who have bought 13.88 times the allotted quota. Qualified institutional buyers also participated in the offer on the first day, picking 1.13 times the portion set aside for them.

Dhanlaxmi Crop Science on December 6 raised Rs 6.37 crore via anchor book at a price of Rs 55 per share. The company has allocated 11.58 lakh equity shares to five institutional investors including Vikasa India EIF I Fund, Eminence Global Fund, and Abudantia Capital.

The Gujarat-based company intends to utilise net fresh issue proceeds (excluding offer expenses) for working capital requirements, and general corporate purposes.

With operations in five states, Dhanlaxmi Crop produced seeds for 24 different field crops and vegetables including cotton, wheat, bajra, maize, gram, okra, green gram, soyabean, mustard, groundnut, and castor. In September 2024, it produced 725MT of raw seeds, comprising hybrids and open pollinated variety of field crops and vegetables. The sale of cotton seeds contributed the maximum to revenue at 77 percent in FY24 and 96 percent in the first of FY25.

The public issue will close on December 11.
data.author: MoneyControl

data.title: "Ignorance of law cannot be an excuse": SEBI fines Premier Polyfilm for related-party transactions
data.description:
The SEBI order said that, being a listed entity since 1994, the company is reasonably expected to understand the nuances of compliances.

Ignorance of law cannot be an excuse said the market regulator while fining Premier Polyfilm Rs 3 lakh for not taking approvals from its audit committee and shareholders for related-party transactions (RPTs).

In an order passed on December 9, Monday, the Securities and Exchange Board of India's (SEBI's) adjudicating officer Asha Shetty wrote, "as a listed entity, Noticee did not conduct as per required prudence in carrying out such RPTs and therefore, violated the provisions of LODR (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements)".

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

The company had entered into RPTs s between April 1, 2022, to March 31, 2023 and April 01, 2023 to May 21, 2023 without taking prior approval its of Audit Committee.

The company submitted that it believed that the said entities were not related parties, since the entities did not fall under the ambit of “related parties” as per the definition prescribed in the Companies Act, 2013. The company added that it obtained the necessary approval after its statutory auditor pointed out the error.

To this, the SEBI order said that the company cannot cite ignorance of law as an excuse.

Also read: Experts suggest strict rules to clean up SME IPO arena; seek lock-in clauses, entry barriers

The order said, "the Noticee (Premier Polyfilm) being a listed company since 1994 is reasonably expected to understand the nuances of compliances. Being a listed company for two decades, Noticee cannot plea ignorance of law. I note that it is a cardinal principle of law that, ‘Ignorantia juris non excusat'."

The company also entered into an RPT, the value of which exceeded 10 percent of its turnover, in FY23 but sought shareholder approval for this transaction only in September 2023 with retrospective effect.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

To this too, the company submitted that it was not aware that RMG Polyvinyl is a related party.

Again the SEBI order said that the company, being a compliant listed company so far, is not expected to feign ignorance of law.

The company was found to have made wrong submissions in halfyearly statements of RPTs submitted to the Exchange in FY 2022-23 with respect to the value of RPTs entered with RMG Polyvinyl. The company submitted that it was under the belief that RMG Polyvinyl was not a related party and therefore did not make the requisite disclosure in the half yearly statements of RPTs submitted to the exchange.
data.author: MoneyControl

data.title: Experts suggest strict rules to clean up SME IPO arena; seek lock-in clauses, entry barriers
data.description:
A total of 225 SMEs launched their IPOs till November with the total fund raising pegged at Rs 8,212 crore, as per data from Prime Database

Capital markets regulator Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI) may well be trying to strengthen the SME IPO arena with newer norms but market participants believe that the watchdog needs to take strict and tough measures to stem the activities that are bringing the segment under regulatory scanner at regular intervals.

While SEBI has issued a consultation paper while proposing a hike in minimum application size among other things, experts told Moneycontrol that the regulator should look at creating entry barriers in the segment while also bringing in lock-in clauses for all IPO investors and pushing for at least a five-fold rise in the minimum application size.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

“The recent past has clearly shown that SME IPO segment is risky for small retail investors and hence SEBI should allow only accredited investors to invest and participate in the SME platform,” said an investment banker wishing not to be named as the discussions are yet internal.

“The concept of accredited investors was designed by SEBI and includes checks and balances to filter entities based on net worth, income and risk appetite. That concept should be used in the SME segment given the recent developments,” he added.

For an entity to be registered as an accredited investor, it needs to have a net worth of at least Rs 25 crore – for individuals the requirement is of a liquid net worth of Rs 5 crore and a total annual income of Rs 50 lakh. Further, there are other criteria as well including some years of experience and an existing portfolio.

Meanwhile, experts have also suggested that SEBI should bring in a minimum of 15 days of lock-in for all IPO investors so that entities who avail of margin financing only with the aim of listing gains can be eliminated or at least filtered.

Incidentally, a recent analysis by SEBI had shown that more than half of the investors in IPOs between April 2021 and December 2023 sold the shares within a week of listing.

“The recent trend on the SME platform has clearly shown that there is a lot of volatility and action in the stocks only on the first couple of days. It shows that investors are coming only for listing gains and such entities often depend on margin financing. If we need genuine investors then a lock-in of 10-15 days can be tried to see if that leads to more genuine investors coming in the segment,” said a source who has been part of the listed SME ecosystem.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Many in the market also feel that the regulator should impose a steep five-fold hike in the minimum application size in the SME segment. Currently, it is pegged at Rs 1 lakh, which was set more than a decade back.

To be sure, the SEBI consultation paper released last month to review the norms for SME segment did propose an increase in the minimum application size. It suggested an increase in the minimum application size to Rs 2 lakh per application, among other recommendations.

Market participants, however, say that mere doubling the limit to Rs 2 lakh will not achieve much as it is still not a significant amount to deter small investors.

The current calendar year has been the best ever in terms of the number of SME IPOs and the cumulative amount raised through such public issues. A total of 225 SMEs launched their IPOs till November with the total fund raising pegged at Rs 8,212 crore, as per data from Prime Database.
data.author: MoneyControl

data.title: Emerald Tyre IPO allotment status: How to check details online via registrar, NSE; Check latest GMP
data.description:
Emerald Tyre Manufacturers has garnered overwhelming response from investors in its 3-day subscription window.

Emerald Tyre Manufacturers shares are likely to be allotted to successful bidders on December 10 following a subscription of nearly 500 times on the closing day of the share sale on Monday. The issue had a price band of Rs 90-95 per share.

Those who subscribed to the maiden share sale by the Emerald Tyre Manufacturers can check their allotment status on the official website of the registrar, Link Intime India Pvt Ltd, using their application number or PAN details.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Emerald Tyre Manufacturers IPO allotment date today: Steps to check share allotment status on Link Intime

Step 1: Open the link to the official registrar of the issue on this URL: https://linkintime.co.in/initial_offer/

Step 2: Select the company from the dropdown menu.

Step 3: You check your allotment status by filling in details like PAN, application number or DP client ID.

Step 4: Press the Submit button

Step 5: Your allotment status will be shown in the window.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad
Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Emerald Tyre Manufacturers SME IPO sees over 487 times subscription on final day, GMP soars

Emerald Tyre Manufacturers IPO GMP Today Price

The Emerald Tyre IPO shares attracted healthy premium in the grey market, in fact, soaring to 100 percent from 75-80 percent quoted last week, the market observers said. According to the current trends, the shares could list at a premium of 90 percent. The grey market is an unofficial platform wherein IPO shares can be bought and sold till the listing.

The trading in Emerald shares will commence on the NSE Emerge platform on December 12.

Incorporated in 2002, the off highway tyre manufacturer makes tyres for material handling applications (like forklifts, skid loaders, ground support equipment of airports, port trailers, agri implements, lawn and garden mowers, mining equipment, aerial work platform trucks, backhoe loaders), under the brand GRECKSTER.

data.title: Technical View: Nifty could rally post-ongoing consolidation if it defends 24,500; Bank Nifty needs to hold 53,000
data.description:
Nifty Technical View

The Nifty 50 continued to consolidate, with minor profit booking for another session on December 9, as participants may be waiting for fresh triggers to determine the market's next direction. As long as the index defends 24,500 on a closing basis, an upward rally is likely in the upcoming sessions. On the higher side, the crucial hurdle is placed at 24,700 (the midpoint of the Bollinger Bands), as above this level, 25,000 becomes the key level to watch. However, if the index falls below 24,500, it may take support at 24,200, followed by 24,000, which is considered a key support level, experts said.

The Nifty 50 remained rangebound throughout the session. It touched an intraday high of 24,705 and a low of 24,580, before closing at 24,619, down 59 points. The index formed a small bearish candle, resembling a Doji-type candlestick pattern, indicating indecision between buyers and sellers.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

According to Shrikant Chouhan, Head of Equity Research at Kotak Securities, the current market environment is non-directional. Hence, level-based trading would be the ideal strategy for day traders, he advised.

For the short term, he said the 24,500-24,450 zone would serve as the immediate support area, while 24,700-24,775 could act as crucial resistance for traders. However, if the index falls below 24,450, traders may prefer to exit long positions, he cautioned.

Weekly options data indicated that the Nifty 50 is likely to trade within the range of 24,000-25,000, with immediate resistance at 24,700 and support at 24,500.

On the Call side, the 25,500 strike has the maximum open interest, followed by the 25,000 and 24,700 strikes, with maximum writing at the 24,700 strike, then at the 25,000 and 25,500 strikes. On the Put side, the maximum open interest was seen at the 24,000 strike, followed by the 24,500 and 24,600 strikes, with maximum writing at the 24,600 strike, followed by the 24,000 and 23,700 strikes.

Bank Nifty

The Bank Nifty also saw minor profit booking for the second consecutive session, closing 102 points lower at 53,408. The index formed a Gravestone Doji-type candlestick pattern on the daily charts, which is considered a bearish reversal pattern. However, traders must wait for the next session before drawing any conclusions. Additionally, a Tweezer Top pattern (although not a classical one) formed in the previous session (Friday), which is also considered a bearish reversal pattern. Therefore, participants need to be cautious.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

"Bank Nifty must hold above the 53,250 zone for an upward move toward 54,000, followed by the previous lifetime high of 54,467. On the downside, support has shifted higher to 53,250 and then to 53,000," said Chandan Taparia, Senior Vice President and Head of Technical Research and Derivatives at Motilal Oswal Financial Services.

Volatility further declined and remained below all key moving averages, which is favourable for bulls. The India VIX, the fear indicator, decreased by 0.23 percent to 14.11, down from 14.14 levels.

Disclaimer: The views and investment tips expressed by investment experts on Moneycontrol.com are their own and not those of the website or its management. Moneycontrol.com advises users to check with certified experts before taking any investment decisions.
data.author: MoneyControl

data.title: GIC picks JPMorgan to advise on $5 billion Greenko stake sale
data.description:
JP Morgan Chase & Co. signage.

GIC Pte is working with JPMorgan Chase & Co. to help advise on a strategic review of options including a sale of its 50% stake in India’s Greenko Energy Holdings, people familiar with the matter said, in what could be one of the biggest disposals in the country.

The Singapore state investor is moving ahead with preparations for a potential deal that could involve a full or partial sale of its holding in Greenko, the people said, asking not to be identified because the deliberations are private. Some investment funds and strategic firms have expressed preliminary interest, the people said.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Considerations are preliminary and GIC could still decide against pursuing a deal, the people said. Representatives for GIC and JPMorgan declined to comment.

Bloomberg News reported in October that GIC was considering options for its 50% stake in Greenko, including a sale. A deal could value the renewable energy firm at about $10 billion, people familiar with the matter have said.

Greenko, which has other backers including Abu Dhabi Investment Authority, known as ADIA, and Japanese financial group Orix Corp., may also seek to raise funds in the coming months to help its growth, a person familiar with the matter has said.

Dealmaking activity in India has jumped this year amid interest from global investors seeking to tap into the country’s growth potential. The volumes of deals involving Indian firms this year has increased 23% from a year earlier, according to data compiled by Bloomberg.

Greenko has 7.5 gigawatts of net installed capacity across wind, solar and hydro power in 15 states in India. Last year, it raised $700 million from GIC, ADIA, Orix and the company’s founders to invest in pumped storage projects.
data.author: MoneyControl

data.title: Emerald Tyre Manufacturers SME IPO sees over 458 times subscription on final day, GMP soars
data.description:
Emerald Tyre Manufacturers IPO

The initial share sale of Tamil Nadu-based Emerald Tyre Manufacturers has garnered overwhelming response from investors on December 9, the final day of bidding, subscribing over 458 times, while the grey market premium also soared significantly.

The tyre manufacturer targets to raise Rs 49.26 crore through the offer, which consists of fresh issue of 49.86 lakh shares worth Rs 47.37 crore, and an offer-for-sale of 1.99 lakh shares worth Rs 1.89 crore by existing shareholders.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Investors have applied for 170.61 crore equity shares as against the offer size of 37.21 lakh shares through 7.84 lakh applications, the subscription data on the NSE showed.

Non-institutional and retail investors took the lead to boost the IPO subscription numbers, picking 882.7 times and 510.2 times of their allotted quotas, while the part set aside for qualified institutional buyers was subscribed 191.14 times so far.

Considering the robust response from investors, the Emerald Tyre IPO shares attracted healthy premium in the grey market, in fact, soaring to 100 percent from 75-80 percent quoted last week, the market observers said. The grey market is an unofficial platform wherein IPO shares can be bought and sold till the listing.

Click Here To Read All IPO News

The trading in Emerald shares will commence on the NSE Emerge platform on December 12.

Incorporated in 2002, the off highway tyre manufacturer makes tyres for material handling applications (like forklifts, skid loaders, ground support equipment of airports, port trailers, agri implements, lawn and garden mowers, mining equipment, aerial work platform trucks, backhoe loaders), under the brand GRECKSTER.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad
Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Also read: International Gemmological Institute India sets price band of Rs 397-417 a share for IPO

The company is going to utilise fresh issue proceeds for its capital expenditure, general corporate purposes, and offer expenses.
